answer:
\boxed{A}

The study demonstrates the feasibility of combining emergency double-balloon enteroscopy (DBE) with real-time capsule endoscopy (CE) in managing acute overt-obscure gastrointestinal bleeding (OGIB), as evidenced by successful lesion detection (77.8% treatable cases) and management modifications in 25% of patients. The high Dieulafoy’s lesion detection rate (40.7%) within 24 hours further supports its utility in urgent settings. These findings validate the combined approach as a practical strategy.
